flags.reason: 2 placeholder rows per filing: 'In 2007 and 2009...from two parties'; each signed CA separately

ai_only_rows.source_quote: In 2007 and 2009, the Company received unsolicited indications of interest to acquire Penford from two parties engaged in the Company’s industry. In each case, the Company entered into a confidentiality agreement and engaged in discussions and exchanged information with the party expressing interest. These discussions did not result in offers to acquire the Company.

ai_only_rows.additional_note: Voluntary drop implied: discussions did not result in offers

flags.reason: filing: 'These discussions did not result in offers to acquire the Company'; no explicit end date; anchored to 2007 year-only inference

In each case, the Company entered into a confidentiality agreement and engaged in discussions and exchanged information with the party expressing interest. These discussions did not result in offers to acquire the Company.

ai_only_rows.source_quote: In 2007 and 2009, the Company received unsolicited indications of interest to acquire Penford from two parties engaged in the Company’s industry. In each case, the Company entered into a confidentiality agreement and engaged in discussions and exchanged information with the party expressing interest. These discussions did not result in offers to acquire the Company.

ai_only_rows.additional_note: Voluntary: 'decided not to move forward with discussions or sign a nondisclosure agreement'
ai_only_rows.source_quote: On September 12, 2014, Party B informed Deutsche Bank that it had decided not to move forward with discussions or sign a nondisclosure agreement with respect to a potential transaction.

ai_only_rows.additional_note: Voluntary: 'combination...was not a suitable strategic fit and declined to pursue further discussions'
ai_only_rows.source_quote: On September 29, 2014, Party F communicated to Deutsche Bank that a combination with Penford was not a suitable strategic fit and declined to pursue further discussions regarding a transaction.

ai_only_rows.additional_note: Target-initiated: Party A's lower indication of value reported at board; board approved Ingredion transaction instead
ai_only_rows.source_quote: Deutsche Bank reported that as part of the market check process, it had contacted a total of six potential counterparties, two of which declined to enter into nondisclosure agreements, one of which never returned Deutsche Bank’s voicemail regarding a potential transaction and the remaining three of which either did not move forward in a timely manner or made a lower indication of value.

ai_only_rows.additional_note: Implicit target-initiated drop: Party C signed NDA 9/15, received presentation 9/25, but never submitted bid; among 'remaining three... either did not move forward in a timely manner or made a lower indication of value'

flags.reason: Party C's implicit drop not explicitly narrated; anchored to 10/14 board meeting when Deutsche Bank reported market-check outcome

ai_only_rows.source_quote: Deutsche Bank reported that as part of the market check process, it had contacted a total of six potential counterparties, two of which declined to enter into nondisclosure agreements, one of which never returned Deutsche Bank’s voicemail regarding a potential transaction and the remaining three of which either did not move forward in a timely manner or made a lower indication of value.

alex_only_rows.comments: [EVERYTHING IN GREY SHOULD NOT BE HERE]

flags.reason: §P-R5/§E4: resolved_name='Ingredion Incorporated' for 'bidder_03' not in aliases_observed=['Ingredion']

ai_rows.source_quote: On July 17, 2014, Mr. Malkoski and Ms. Gordon met for lunch, during which meeting Ms. Gordon advised Mr. Malkoski of Ingredion’s interest in acquiring Penford.

ai_rows.additional_note: Party A's representative sent email requesting a call; purpose not disclosed
ai_rows.source_quote: On August 1, 2014, a representative acting on behalf of a company in the industry (referred to as Party A) sent Mr. Malkoski an email asking for a call. On August 4, 2014, Mr. Malkoski and the representative acting on behalf of Party A discussed scheduling a meeting with the Chief Executive Officer of Party A for August 11, 2014. Party A’s representative did not indicate the purpose of the meeting.

ai_rows.additional_note: Party A CEO informally discussed Party A's potential interest in acquiring or combining with Penford
ai_rows.source_quote: Also on August 11, 2014, Mr. Malkoski met with the Chief Executive Officer of Party A. Mr. Malkoski and Party A’s Chief Executive Officer briefly discussed the recent SEACOR Filing. Party A’s Chief Executive Officer also informally discussed Party A’s potential interest in acquiring or combining with Penford. Party A noted that it saw value in Penford but only at a typical takeover premium based on current trading price, due to the fact that it would base any offer price on Penford’s performance over the last twelve months, instead of Penford’s strategic plan and any synergies that could be realized in the future.

ai_rows.additional_note: Market-check counterparty; expressed interest but never signed NDA
ai_rows.source_quote: Also on September 9, 2014, another strategic counterparty contacted by Deutsche Bank (referred to as Party B) expressed interest in further discussions, and Deutsche Bank forwarded a nondisclosure and standstill agreement to proceed with additional discussions with Party B.

ai_rows.additional_note: Re-engaged through Deutsche Bank market check; NDA forwarded

flags.reason: Party A previously contacted Penford directly on 8/1; Deutsche Bank re-engages as part of market check

ai_rows.source_quote: On September 9, 2014, Party A expressed interest in having further discussions regarding a transaction and Deutsche Bank forwarded Party A a nondisclosure and standstill agreement.

ai_rows.additional_note: Market-check counterparty expressed interest; NDA forwarded 9/11
ai_rows.source_quote: On September 10, 2014, a third potential strategic counterparty contacted by Deutsche Bank (referred to as Party C) expressed interest in pursuing a transaction, and Deutsche Bank forwarded Party C a nondisclosure and standstill agreement on September 11, 2014.

ai_rows.additional_note: Market-check counterparty expressed interest; NDA forwarded
ai_rows.source_quote: On September 11, 2014, a fourth potential strategic counterparty contacted by Deutsche Bank (referred to as Party D) expressed interest, and Deutsche Bank forwarded a nondisclosure and standstill agreement to Party D.

ai_rows.additional_note: Market-check counterparty contacted by Deutsche Bank; never signed NDA
ai_rows.source_quote: On September 24, 2014, in accordance with the board’s authorization, Deutsche Bank contacted Party F regarding a potential transaction with Penford.

ai_rows.additional_note: Executive Committee directed retention 7/24; engagement letter executed 2014-09-11
ai_rows.source_quote: Following discussion of potential financial advisors, the Executive Committee directed management to proceed to retain Deutsche Bank as the Company’s financial advisor and to advise on shareholder matters and to evaluate strategic alternatives, and directed management to negotiate a suitable engagement letter. An engagement letter with Deutsche Bank was executed on September 11, 2014.

ai_rows.additional_note: Acquirer-side IB; filing does not narrate a retention date — anchored to first narration per §J1

flags.reason: filing does not narrate retention date; inferred from first mention per §J1

ai_rows.source_quote: On August 21, 2014, members of Penford’s management team provided a presentation to Ms. Gordon, Mr. Fortnum, and certain other members of Ingredion’s senior management and representatives of J.P. Morgan Securities, Ingredion’s financial advisor.

alex_rows.comments: Legal counsel: Perkins Coie | It looks like DB was already seleected on July 30, and provided some advice on Aug 11, even though an engagement letter was signed on Sep 11. Which date should be used?
